content.problemStatement:
Bowling balls come in 111111 total sizes, an integer weight between 666 and 161616 (measured in pounds).

In a bowling event, Chef is given a total of NNN bowling balls, with the iii-th one being AiA_iAi​ pounds. Some of the bowling balls may have the same weight as the others.

Chef can use a bowling ball which is at least XXX pounds, and at most YYY pounds. Find the number of bowling balls Chef can use.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains 333 integers - N,XN, XN,X and YYY.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of bowling balls Chef can use.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Chef can use the second and third bowling balls.
Test Case 2 : Chef can use all the bowling balls.
Test Case 3 : Chef can use none of the bowling balls.
